If long pepper, black pepper, dried ginger, cyperus grass,
Lodhra, costus, cloth, water, cumin seeds, Gandhamarhsi, Sonph or
Tagara be seen or mentioned at the time, the querist's thoughts
would be about a woman's failure, a man's fault, an afflicted person,
loss of everything, missing of the passage, loss of children, loss of
money, destruction of corn, son's death, loss of bipeds (animals
with cloven hoofs), loss of quadrupeds and destruction of landed
property respectively.

In the third line *n*W is another reading for The metre
of the first is
If the querist holds in his hand the fruits of the banyan tree,
Madhuka, Tinduka, Jambu, Plaksa, mangoes, and jujube, he would
gain wealth, gold, servants, iron, silken clothes, silver and copper
respectively.
